'''Bobot Beka''' was a [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A lowly thug, Beka initially worked for an individual named [[Petson Lizze]] for some [[Time/Legends|time]] but by [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] was no longer in her employment. A [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was subsequently placed on his head by the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] administrator of [[Outland Transit Station]], [[Rozatta]], for vandalizing her transport skiffs with [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|rebel]] graffiti. She offered 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his capture alive, but only 1,000 credits if he was brought in [[Death/Legends|dead]].

The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] ran into Beka on [[Merchant Row (Outland Station)|Merchant Row]] in 32 BBY, where the criminal recognized and attacked him with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] along with several other thugs. Fett, who was on the station chasing Beka's new employer, [[Muttani]] criminal [[Meeko Ghintee]], had also been informed by Rozatta herself of Beka's bounty and claimed it, capturing the vandal alive for 2,000 credits.

==Behind the scenes==
Bobot Beka first appeared in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', which was released by [[LucasArts]] in [[2002]]. Beka was one of several secondary bounties that players could optionally capture on the second level of Chapter One. Players could capture Beka alive or dead, or not capture him at all; however, this article assumes that Fett captured him alive as this is the highest paying option.
